tests.intent: Relational definitions cover every reviewed ammo, weapon, pickup, key, armor, power, projectile, barrel, weapon-state, and muzzle-state behavior without procedural type dispatch.
Every one of the 23 distinct interactive pickup, weapon, key, and barrel thing types placed in reviewed E1M1 has an independently identified focused replay and consumption result.
Four ammo families clamp at normal caps, a backpack doubles each cap exactly once, full resources reject redundant pickup consumption, and overflow cannot occur.
Slot selection requires relational ownership and sufficient ammo, transitions through lower and raise states, and never trusts a client-selected weapon identifier.
A weapon without its required ammo cannot be selected or fired, consumes no ammo or random value, creates no attack, and emits only the reviewed dry-fire result.
Held fire follows exact definition tics through fire, flash, refire, ready, lowering, and raising states while counting refire deterministically and respecting release.
Spread and damage consume the database Doom random table in fixed pellet and attack order with exact cursor advancement and no random read on rejected attacks.
Hitscan uses exact intersection ordering to damage only the stable nearest shootable intercept, with deterministic identifier tie breaking and no penetration to farther targets.
A one-sided or closed-opening wall intercept before a target blocks every pellet, while a valid open portal permits the independently expected nearest target hit.
Shotgun fire consumes one shell, emits exactly seven independently spread pellets, performs two spread and one damage draw per pellet, and aggregates exact damage.
Fist and chainsaw attacks require range and line of sight, consume no ammo, apply deterministic damage, use reviewed refire states, and respect berserk scaling only for fist.
Rocket and plasma attacks spend exact ammo, spawn database-owned mobjs at reviewed muzzle origin with fixed momentum and owner, then advance once per logical tic.
Swept projectile collision selects the earliest wall or shootable actor, prevents tunneling and owner self-hit during launch, emits one impact, and removes the projectile once.
Rocket and barrel splash use exact bounded distance falloff, stable victim ordering, radius boundary behavior, owner damage, and line-of-effect occlusion by blocking geometry.
A destroyed E1M1 barrel explodes exactly once, its splash can deterministically ignite later barrels, chain events retain stable ordinals, and cycles cannot recurse twice.
Stimpack and medikit stop at 100, bonuses and soulsphere reach but never exceed 200, redundant capped pickups remain present, and berserk establishes reviewed health.
Armor bonuses cap at 200, green and blue armor only replace weaker protection, armor classes absorb reviewed damage shares, and health receives the exact remainder.
Blue key and berserk ownership persist in authoritative player state, duplicates do not inflate counters, useful pickups consume once, and inert capped pickups remain available.
Each E1M1 interactive type and each weapon repeats from identical state and commands with identical rows, event ordinals, random cursor, logical state hash, and frame hash.
Pickup, selection, refire, hitscan, projectile, splash, barrel chain, armor, key, and power transitions compose over a multi-tic replay without starving world machines.
All mutations are session-scoped inside the owning tic transaction, preserve other sessions, propagate errors, roll back atomically, and never commit autonomously.
Production uses relational joins and reviewed geometry while rejecting evaluator reads, fixture answers, host inspection, dynamic SQL, host randomness, wall time, and embedded E1M1 identifiers.
Every isolated semantic defect has a focused independent witness spanning caps, consumption, selection, refire, random order, intercepts, projectiles, splash, and barrel chaining.
